verdict_reasoning: EREG is an EGF family member initially expressed as a membrane-anchored proform, but the functional, biologically relevant form is shed by ADAM-family proteases as a 46-aa soluble ligand. After cleavage, mature EREG is secreted and circulates as a free ligand to activate distant EGFR/ERBB4 receptors; it is not stably anchored to the plasma membrane. The protein body (mature EREG) does not reach the outer leaflet via its own mechanism post-secretion—it is released into extracellular space only.
